title: Executive Order 11888—Implementing the Generalized System of Preferences

flags.justification: Exercises specific, statutorily-defined presidential designation authority (Trade Act §§502-503), with required procedural predecessors (public comment, ITC advice, congressional notification) already completed per the order's own recitals.
Extremely detailed technical amendments with precise cross-references to exact tariff schedule item numbers and numeric value-added thresholds.
Multiple technical provisions all implement one statute (Title V of the Trade Act of 1974).
Eligibility criteria are precise, objective, numeric standards (35% and 50% value-added thresholds).
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.

Standing-procedural-framework category strongly applies: this order supersedes a prior order (EO 11844) issued less than a year earlier, directly evidencing an active, ongoing pattern of periodic revision.
The order's own recitals confirm the statutorily-required congressional notification was completed before these designations were made.
The objective, numeric eligibility criteria are checkable and auditable by customs authorities.
Routine, technical implementation of an already-established, congressionally-designed trade preference program; no novel authority claim.
No conflicting agency mandate is implicated.
The 'A*' exclusion list (Sec. 9(c)(iii)) reflects the GSP statute's own built-in competitive-need limitation, specifically enumerated rather than a broad, undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/20 = 0.0%. Extremely well-specified, numerically precise trade-policy implementation.
